crime.value: Assassination of mayor by sniper from balcony; question: who was the shooter and why target the mayor?
crime.quote: The mayor was getting out of his Rolls Royce when someone shot him from a balcony overhead.

victim.value: The mayor (Pellistan) - target unclear from text, no specific motive for targeting him revealed
victim.quote: I need you to figure out who shot the mayor last week.

culprit_and_motive.value: Thomas Hinckly (FBI Section Chief) wanting John Henning's higher position, possibly with help from corrupt Deputy Director Charles Davids
culprit_and_motive.quote: Hinckly wanted Hennings's position, and decided to do something about it. He bribed Charles Davids

resolution.value: Private investigator deduces the conspiracy through research and calls police hotline to report findings
resolution.quote: I called the information hotline number given on the news. 'Hello?' I said into the phone. 'I may know who killed the mayor.'

investigator.value: Brooke L, private investigator hired by supposed FBI agent to solve the case
investigator.quote: Being a private investigator, or PI, I was always ready for someone to trick me.

suspect_architecture.value: Three primary suspects: Thomas Hinckly (FBI Section Chief with career motive), John Henning (superior officer), and Charles Davids (Deputy Director with bribery history). Professional hierarchy creates means/motive spread.
suspect_architecture.quote: Hinckly was a Section Chief in the FBI, one rank below Henning. Right above Henning was the Deputy Director

red_herring.value: The anonymous FBI caller positioning himself as client rather than suspect, using the classic misdirection of the reporter never being considered the killer.
red_herring.quote: Besides that, the man who reported the case is never thought to be the killer.

clue_fairness.value: Physical evidence shared with reader: FBI badge authentication, tie-tack camera photos, certificate details, and news photo of killer's appearance all provided before PI's deduction.
clue_fairness.quote: Inconspicuously, the 14-megapixel camera in my tie tack snapped a photograph of the medallion.

social_world.value: FBI institutional hierarchy with corruption at Deputy Director level, where rank ambition and bribery networks enable crime and cover-up within law enforcement power structure.
social_world.quote: Davids had been in lots of trouble for taking bribes.

alibi.value: Hinckly's implicit alibi as the FBI agent reporting the crime dissolves when physical evidence (news photo showing different appearance) contradicts his positioning as investigator rather than suspect.
alibi.quote: He had black hair and tan skin. [The killer had] light brown hair and fair skin

reveal_mechanic.value: Visual comparison between news photograph of killer's physical appearance and suspect database photos reveals the deception through contradictory physical evidence.
reveal_mechanic.quote: The killer had light brown hair and fair skin, and was well built. He had black hair and tan skin.

media_and_audience.value: News media coverage provides crucial evidence through released crime scene photos, while PI recognizes the institutional implications for public trust in FBI.
media_and_audience.quote: it would change the people's perspective on the FBI dramatically, and make them wonder how safe they really were

investigator_wound.value: PI's professional paranoia about being tricked by revenge-seeking criminals makes him vulnerable to the substantial bribe, compromising his investigative objectivity.
investigator_wound.quote: Being a private investigator, or PI, I was always ready for someone to trick me. People get caught because of me.
